response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering skills, TDD discipline, and experience with complex systems, they lack the core technical requirements for this role. Their career is heavily focused on TypeScript/Node.js and modern web stacks, with only minimal Java exposure (one robotic arm project) and zero Kotlin experience. The absence of Spring ecosystem knowledge and enterprise framework experience—which are explicitly required—represents a critical gap that would require extensive ramp-up time for a senior-level position.
strengths: Proven experience designing and maintaining complex large-scale systems (microservices handling millions of requests, distributed platforms)
Strong software craftsmanship practices with emphasis on TDD, clean architecture, and extensible design across multiple projects
Demonstrated IDE/tooling experience from National Instruments work on LabWindows/CVI IDE and multiple open-source development tools
concerns: Minimal Java experience (single robotic arm project at Altom) with no mention of Kotlin—the role requires strong expertise in both languages
Zero experience with Spring ecosystem, Quarkus, Hibernate, or other enterprise frameworks that are central to the position
Career trajectory heavily weighted toward TypeScript/Node.js and modern web development rather than JVM-focused enterprise tooling

key_factors.factor: Software architecture and systems design capability
